narrative:
In the afternoon at Tilden Regional Park, a group of Chestnut-backed Chickadees was heard darting through oak woodland, while a single Common Raven could be seen soaring over open ridgelines. A few Orange-crowned Warblers also joined in the chorus, their muted tones blending with other avian sounds under clear skies.

The Chestnut-backed Chickadee is a new entry in this monitoring window.
